LHX.sentence: L3Harris slipped almost 6% and its profit margins are running thinner than the bigger defense companies', though steady military spending means the underlying business is still intact.

ASML.sentence: ASML has run up quite a bit lately and sits well above its recent average price — nothing wrong, just stretched after a strong stretch for the only company that makes these advanced chipmaking machines.

LIN.sentence: Linde is quiet this week, doing what it always does — selling industrial gases on long contracts and nudging prices up a little at a time.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is barely moving but is quietly cheaper than its big tech peers right now, which is unusual for a company with this much going for it in cloud computing and AI.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is down nearly 28% from its high as lab and biotech spending sits near a low point, which makes this maker of essential research equipment look unusually cheap.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady, but its dividend payout isn't much better than a safe government bond these days, so it's looking a bit expensive for what you get.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow has fallen more than half from its peak, a steep drop for a company whose workflow-automation and AI tools keep getting signed up by big corporations and the federal government.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is down slightly and its earnings are at a deep low point, which for a mining and trading company usually signals the bottom of a cycle rather than an expensive stock.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is down again and now sits well below its longer-term average, near the low end of its year — a rough stretch after its earlier run.

KOF.sentence: The Latin American Coca-Cola bottler ticked up a bit and looks cheap for a steady, reliable business that pays a decent dividend.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is basically flat and trading well below its recent average, but with oil prices healthy the weakness looks more about Brazilian politics than the actual business.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the container shipping giant, dropped about 3% and is now valued below the worth of its own ships — the market is essentially giving its logistics business away for free.
